get.description:
List all available features and their subfeatures.

Returns features that exist in both the registry (technically supported)
and database (have available providers).

List all subfeatures for a specific feature.

Args:
    feature: Feature name (e.g., 'text', 'image', 'ocr', 'translation')

Returns:
    Feature info with display names and list of subfeatures.

Get detailed information for a specific feature/subfeature.

Args:
    feature: Feature name (e.g., 'text', 'image', 'ocr', 'translation')
    subfeature: Subfeature name (e.g., 'ai_detection', 'ocr', 'generation')
    format: Schema output format (simplified or json_schema)

Returns:
    - Feature and subfeature display names
    - Input schema
    - Output schema
    - Available providers and their models

parameters.name: format

schema.description: Schema format: 'simplified' for flat readable format, 'json_schema' for full Pydantic JSON Schema

parameters.description: Schema format: 'simplified' for flat readable format, 'json_schema' for full Pydantic JSON Schema

get.description: Retrieve a stored model response by ID.

delete.description: Delete a stored model response by ID.

post.description: Anthropic Messages API — native pass-through via litellm.
Count input tokens for an Anthropic-format request without running the model.

OpenAI-compatible image-edit endpoint.

Accepts either ``application/json`` (gpt-image-style ``images: [{file_id|image_url}]``)
or ``multipart/form-data`` (OpenAI SDK classic shape: ``image[]`` UploadFile,
optional ``mask`` UploadFile, plus text fields). Content-Type drives dispatch.

size.title: Size
size.description: Provider-specific size string. OpenAI accepts '1024x1024', '1536x1024', '1024x1536', 'auto'. Vertex Imagen accepts square or aspect-ratio strings. Validation is delegated to the provider.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata attached to the request.

extra_headers.title: Extra Headers
extra_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers forwarded to the provider API. Credential headers (Authorization, x-api-key, ...) are rejected.

images.title: Images
images.description: One or more input images. Each entry references one image via exactly one of `file_id` (an Eden upload id) or `image_url` (https URL or base64 data URL). The first image is the canvas; subsequent images are inpaint references.

mask.description: Optional mask image (PNG with transparent pixels marking the regions to edit). Same shape as an `images[]` entry.

schema.title: ImageEditJsonBody
schema.description:
OpenAI-compatible ``POST /v1/images/edits`` JSON request body.

References:
- OpenAI's current /v1/images/edits JSON form for gpt-image-* takes
  ``images: [{file_id | image_url}, ...]`` and an optional ``mask:
  {file_id | image_url}``.
- ``image_url`` accepts an https URL or a ``data:image/...;base64,...`` URL.

get.description: List image-generation / image-edit models available in the caller's region.
List available LLM models with extended metadata.

delete.description: Invalidate router default-models cache. Staff only.

schema.title: Response Invalidate Router Cache V3 Router Cache Delete

post.description:
Universal AI endpoint for synchronous non-LLM AI features.

Model format: feature/subfeature/provider[/model]

Supported features:
- text/ai_detection, text/moderation, etc.
- ocr/ocr, ocr/identity_parser, etc.
- image/generation, image/background_removal, etc.

For async features (e.g., audio/speech_to_text_async), use POST /v3/universal-ai/async.

Request body:
- model: Model string in format feature/subfeature/provider[/model]
- input: Feature-specific input parameters
- fallbacks: Optional list of fallback provider strings (max 3)
- provider_params: Optional provider-specific parameters
- show_original_response: Include raw provider response (default: false)

Example:
```json
{
    "model": "text/ai_detection/openai/gpt-4",
    "input": {"text": "Analyze this text"}
}
```

Create an async job for long-running AI operations.

Model format: feature/subfeature/provider[/model]

Supported async features:
- audio/speech_to_text_async

Request body:
- model: Model string in format feature/subfeature/provider[/model]
- input: Feature-specific input parameters
- fallbacks: Optional list of fallback provider strings (max 3)
- webhook_receiver: Optional URL to receive job completion notification
- user_webhook_parameters: Optional custom parameters for webhook payload

Returns 202 Accepted with job info for polling.

get.description: List async jobs for the authenticated user.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items per page
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items per page

get.description: Get details for a specific async job.

delete.description: Delete an async job. Users can only delete their own jobs.

schema.title: Response Delete Async Job V3 Universal Ai Async  Job Id  Delete

post.description:
Upload a file for persistent storage.

Returns a file_id that can be used in subsequent API calls to /v3/universal-ai.

Files are stored securely and only accessible by the file owner.
Default expiration is 30 days, maximum 30 days.

get.description:
List uploaded files for the authenticated user.

Optionally filter by purpose. Only returns non-expired files.
Results are ordered by creation date (newest first).

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items per page
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items per page

delete.description:
Delete all uploaded files for the authenticated user.

This permanently deletes all files from storage.
This action cannot be undone.

post.description:
Delete specific files by their IDs.

Only files owned by the authenticated user will be deleted.
Files that don't exist or aren't owned by the user are silently ignored.
This action cannot be undone.

AnthropicCountTokensBody.title: AnthropicCountTokensBody
AnthropicCountTokensBody.description: Body for /v1/messages/count_tokens — same fields as messages but max_tokens is optional.

input_tokens.title: Input Tokens
input_tokens.description: Number of input tokens the request would consume.

fallbacks.title: Fallbacks
fallbacks.description: List of fallback model IDs to try if the primary model fails. Models are tried in order. Example: ['anthropic/claude-3-opus', 'openai/gpt-4o']

router_candidates.title: Router Candidates
router_candidates.description: List of model candidates for dynamic routing when using model='@edenai'. Each entry should be 'provider/model', e.g. ['openai/gpt-4o', 'anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022']. If not provided, defaults to all available models.

type.title: Type
type.description: Object type. Always 'message' for this endpoint.

content.title: Content
content.description: Ordered list of content blocks produced by the model.

stop_sequence.title: Stop Sequence
stop_sequence.description: Custom stop sequence that triggered the stop, if any.

cache_creation_input_tokens.title: Cache Creation Input Tokens
cache_creation_input_tokens.description: Tokens written to the prompt cache (when prompt caching is used).

cache_read_input_tokens.title: Cache Read Input Tokens
cache_read_input_tokens.description: Tokens read from the prompt cache (when prompt caching is used).

AnthropicUsage.title: AnthropicUsage
AnthropicUsage.description: Token usage block returned by the Anthropic Messages API.

type.title: Type
type.description: Cache type. Currently only 'ephemeral' is supported.

CacheControl.title: CacheControl
CacheControl.description:
Anthropic-style cache marker placed on a message content part.

LiteLLM translates this into native provider context caching (e.g. Gemini
``cachedContents``, Anthropic prompt caching). Only a single contiguous
block of cache-marked content is cached per request, and providers enforce
a minimum token count below which caching is silently skipped.

index.title: Index
index.description: Position of the choice in the list.

message.description: Message produced by the model for this choice.

finish_reason.title: Finish Reason
finish_reason.description: Reason the model stopped generating tokens.

logprobs.title: Logprobs
logprobs.description: Log probability information for the choice, if requested.

ChatCompletionChoice.title: ChatCompletionChoice
ChatCompletionChoice.description: A single choice in a chat completion response.

tool_calls.title: Tool Calls
tool_calls.description: Tool calls produced by the model, if any.

refusal.title: Refusal
refusal.description: Refusal message from the model, if any.

reasoning_content.title: Reasoning Content
reasoning_content.description: Reasoning trace from the model, when supported by the provider.

ChatCompletionMessage.title: ChatCompletionMessage
ChatCompletionMessage.description: Message returned in a chat completion choice.

id.title: Id
id.description: Unique identifier for the chat completion.

object.title: Object
object.description: Object type. Always 'chat.completion' for this endpoint.

created.title: Created
created.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the completion was created.

choices.title: Choices
choices.description: List of completion choices generated for the request.

usage.description: Token usage statistics for the request.

system_fingerprint.title: System Fingerprint
system_fingerprint.description: Backend configuration fingerprint that produced the response.

service_tier.title: Service Tier
service_tier.description: Service tier that processed the request.

total_tokens.title: Total Tokens
total_tokens.description: Total tokens consumed by the request.

prompt_tokens_details.title: Prompt Tokens Details
prompt_tokens_details.description: Breakdown of prompt tokens (e.g. cached_tokens, audio_tokens).

ChatCompletionUsage.title: ChatCompletionUsage
ChatCompletionUsage.description: Token usage block returned by the OpenAI-compatible chat completion API.

file_ids.title: File Ids
file_ids.description: List of file IDs to delete (1-100)

DeleteFilesRequest.title: DeleteFilesRequest
DeleteFilesRequest.description: Request body for deleting multiple files.

EmbeddingItem.title: EmbeddingItem
EmbeddingItem.description:
OpenAI embedding item with the wire-format quirk made explicit.

The OpenAI SDK types ``embedding`` as ``list[float]`` only because the SDK
transparently decodes base64 before populating the model. The actual API
*wire* response returns a base64 string when ``encoding_format='base64'``.
Eden is a passthrough for OpenAI-compatible callers who explicitly want the
smaller base64 payload, so we must allow both shapes.

input.title: Input
input.description: Input to embed: string, list of strings, pre-tokenized list of ints, or pre-tokenized batch (list of int lists). Max 2048 items for OpenAI.

dimensions.title: Dimensions
dimensions.description: Reduce output vector size. 3-series models only.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata attached to the request.

extra_headers.title: Extra Headers
extra_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers forwarded to the provider API.

EmbeddingsBody.title: EmbeddingsBody
EmbeddingsBody.description:
OpenAI-compatible `POST /v1/embeddings` request body, plus minimal Eden extensions.

Unknown top-level fields are forwarded to the underlying provider.

EmbeddingsResponse.title: EmbeddingsResponse
EmbeddingsResponse.description:
OpenAI-compatible embeddings response + Eden `cost` and `provider` fields.

`EmbeddingsDataClass` (from edenai-apis) already widens
``data[].embedding`` to ``list[float] | str`` so the base64 wire format
validates here too.

FeatureInfo.title: FeatureInfo
FeatureInfo.description: Info about a feature and its subfeatures.

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: Unique file identifier for use in API calls

aspect_ratio.title: Aspect Ratio
aspect_ratio.description: Output aspect ratio. Examples: '1:1', '16:9', '9:16', '4:3', '3:4', '21:9'. Allowed set varies by model; see Gemini docs.

image_size.title: Image Size
image_size.description: Output resolution. Examples: '1K', '2K', '4K'. '512' is valid on Gemini 3.1 Flash Image only.

GeminiImageConfig.title: GeminiImageConfig
GeminiImageConfig.description:
Image generation config for Gemini image models (google/gemini-2.5-flash-image).

Values are passed through to Gemini's generationConfig.imageConfig. Allowed
values may vary by model — see
https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/image-generation.

ImageDataItem.title: ImageDataItem
ImageDataItem.description:
Single image entry inside the OpenAI-shaped ``data: [...]`` array.

Providers return either ``url`` (most non-OpenAI providers) or
``b64_json`` (gpt-image-*); ``revised_prompt`` is OpenAI-specific.

size.title: Size
size.description: Provider-specific size string. OpenAI accepts '1024x1024', '1536x1024', '1024x1536', 'auto'. Vertex Imagen accepts square or aspect-ratio strings. Validation is delegated to the provider.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata attached to the request.

extra_headers.title: Extra Headers
extra_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers forwarded to the provider API. Credential headers (Authorization, x-api-key, ...) are rejected.

quality.title: Quality
quality.description: Provider-specific quality string (e.g. 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'standard', 'hd', 'auto'). Accepted values depend on the model.

response_format.title: Response Format
response_format.description: Legacy DALL-E parameter. Ignored by gpt-image-* and forwarded to the provider for any model that still honors it.

ImageResponse.title: ImageResponse
ImageResponse.description:
OpenAI-compatible image response + Eden ``cost`` / ``provider`` fields.

Shared by ``POST /v3/images/generations`` and ``POST /v3/images/edits`` —
the wire shape is identical.

ListFilesResponse.title: ListFilesResponse
ListFilesResponse.description: Response for listing files with pagination.

fallbacks.title: Fallbacks
fallbacks.description: List of fallback model IDs to try if the primary model fails. Models are tried in order. Example: ['anthropic/claude-3-opus', 'openai/gpt-4o']

router_candidates.title: Router Candidates
router_candidates.description: List of model candidates for dynamic routing when using model='@edenai'. Each entry should be 'provider/model', e.g. ['openai/gpt-4o', 'anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022']. If not provided, defaults to all available models.

pre_hooks.title: Pre Hooks
pre_hooks.description: List of hooks to run before the LLM provider call. Each entry must have an 'action' key and optional 'params'. Example: [{'action': 'pdf_text_extract', 'params': {'method': 'default'}}]

post_hooks.title: Post Hooks
post_hooks.description: List of hooks to run after the LLM provider call. Each entry must have an 'action' key and optional 'params'. Example: [{'action': 'json_heal', 'params': {'json_schema': {...}}}]

model.title: Model
model.description: The name of the LLM model to use.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: The messages to send to the LLM model.

n.title: N
n.description: The number of completions to generate for each prompt. Defaults to 1.

reasoning_effort.title: Reasoning Effort
reasoning_effort.description: The reasoning effort level for the LLM model.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Object of metadata associated with the chat request. Can be used to provide additional context or tracking information.

frequency_penalty.title: Frequency Penalty
frequency_penalty.description: Penalty for repeated tokens in the output.

logit_bias.title: Logit Bias
logit_bias.description: Logit bias to influence token generation.

logprobs.title: Logprobs
logprobs.description: Whether to include log probabilities of tokens in the output. Defaults to False.

top_logprobs.title: Top Logprobs
top_logprobs.description: Number of top log probabilities to return with each token.

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate in the chat completion

max_completion_tokens.title: Max Completion Tokens
max_completion_tokens.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a completion, including visible output tokens and reasoning tokens.

modalities.title: Modalities
modalities.description: List of supported input/output modalities for the chat.

audio.title: Audio
audio.description: dictionary for audio-related parameters or metadata.

presence_penalty.title: Presence Penalty
presence_penalty.description: Penalty for new tokens based on their presence in the text so far.

response_format.title: Response Format
response_format.description: Specify the desired response format for the completion.

service_tier.title: Service Tier
service_tier.description:
'auto': Automatically select appropriate tier
'default': Use the default service tier
'flex': 50% cheaper processing with increased latency (OpenAI o3/o4-mini)

stop.title: Stop
stop.description: List of stop sequences to end the generation.

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: Whether to stream the response in real-time. Defaults to False.

stream_options.title: Stream Options
stream_options.description: Options for streaming responses, such as chunk size or format.

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: Sampling temperature for controlling randomness in output.

top_p.title: Top P
top_p.description: Nucleus sampling parameter for controlling diversity in output. Defaults to 1.0.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: List of tools that can be used by the model to assist in generating responses.

tool_choice.title: Tool Choice
tool_choice.description: Specify how tools should be used. Can be 'auto', 'required', 'none', or an object to force a specific tool.

parallel_tool_calls.title: Parallel Tool Calls
parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to allow parallel tool calls in the completion.

user.title: User
user.description: User identifier for tracking or personalization purposes.

function_call.title: Function Call
function_call.description: Function call parameters for invoking specific functions during the chat.

functions.title: Functions
functions.description: List of functions that can be called by the model to assist in generating responses.

thinking.title: Thinking
thinking.description: Parameters related to the model's reasoning or thinking process.

web_search_options.title: Web Search Options
web_search_options.description: Options for web search integration. Example: json web_search_options={ "search_context_size": "medium" # Options: "low", "medium", "high" }

verbosity.title: Verbosity
verbosity.description: Hint the model to be more or less expansive in its replies. Values: "low", "medium", "high". low (gpt5 models)

extra_body.title: Extra Body
extra_body.description: Additional parameters to pass in the request body to the provider API.

image_config.description: Image generation configuration for Google Gemini image models (e.g. google/gemini-2.5-flash-image).

extra_headers.title: Extra Headers
extra_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers to pass to the provider API.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the message sender (e.g., 'user', 'assistant').

content.title: Content
content.description: The content of the message, either text or image.

file_data.title: File Data
file_data.description: The file data in base64 format.

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: File identifier: either a UUID from /v3/upload endpoint or an HTTP(S) URL.

text.title: Text
text.description: The text content of the message.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.description: Pricing information after any applicable discounts have been applied

list_pricing.title: List Pricing
list_pricing.description: Provider list pricing, before any discounts are applied

discount.title: Discount
discount.description: Discount applied to the model (0-1 range)

regions.title: Regions
regions.description: Regions where this model is available

ModelObject.title: ModelObject
ModelObject.description: Extended model object with full metadata.

input.title: Input
input.description: Text or multimodal content to classify.

RegionObject.title: RegionObject
RegionObject.description: Region where a model is available.

ResponseInputItem.title: ResponseInputItem
ResponseInputItem.description: Non-message input item forwarded to the provider as-is (reasoning, function_call, etc.).

fallbacks.title: Fallbacks
fallbacks.description: List of fallback model IDs to try if the primary model fails. Models are tried in order. Example: ['anthropic/claude-3-opus', 'openai/gpt-4o']

router_candidates.title: Router Candidates
router_candidates.description: List of model candidates for dynamic routing when using model='@edenai'. Each entry should be 'provider/model', e.g. ['openai/gpt-4o', 'anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022']. If not provided, defaults to all available models.

input.title: Input
input.description: Text, image, or file inputs to the model

instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.description: System/developer instructions prepended to input. Not carried over when using previous_response_id.

previous_response_id.title: Previous Response Id
previous_response_id.description: ID of a prior response to continue a multi-turn conversation. The provider manages conversation state server-side.

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: Whether to stream the response via server-sent events.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: List of tools the model may call (function, web_search, file_search, etc.).

tool_choice.title: Tool Choice
tool_choice.description: Controls which tool is called. 'auto', 'required', 'none', or a specific tool object.

truncation.title: Truncation
truncation.description: How to handle context that exceeds the model's context window.

store.title: Store
store.description: Whether the provider should store the response server-side for later retrieval.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Up to 16 key-value pairs for tagging.

user.title: User
user.description: Stable end-user identifier for abuse detection.

include.title: Include
include.description: Additional output data to include, e.g. 'file_search_call.results'.

background.title: Background
background.description: Whether to run the model response in the background.

SubfeatureDetailResponse.title: SubfeatureDetailResponse
SubfeatureDetailResponse.description: Detailed response for a specific subfeature.

input.title: Input

examples.text: Analyze this text for AI detection

fallbacks.title: Fallbacks
fallbacks.description:
Fallback providers to try if the primary provider fails, in order. Accepted formats:
  - 'provider' or 'provider/model' (e.g. 'amazon', 'openai/gpt-4')
  - full model id (e.g 'text/moderation/google', 'image/generation/minimax/image-01')

show_original_response.title: Show Original Response
show_original_response.description: Include raw provider response in the output

webhook_receiver.title: Webhook Receiver
webhook_receiver.description: Webhook URL to receive job completion notification

user_webhook_parameters.title: User Webhook Parameters
user_webhook_parameters.description: Custom parameters to include in webhook payload

UniversalAIAsyncBody.title: UniversalAIAsyncBody
UniversalAIAsyncBody.description:
Universal AI async request body.

Extends UniversalAIBody with webhook parameters for async notifications.

status.title: Status
status.description: Request status: success, fail, or processing (async only)

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: Cost in credits for this request

provider.title: Provider
provider.description: Provider name that processed the request

error.title: Error
error.description: Error details from the provider (only present when status is 'fail')

original_response.title: Original Response
original_response.description: Raw response from the provider (if show_original_response=true)

model.title: Model
model.description: Model name if specified in the request

UniversalAIAsyncResponse.title: UniversalAIAsyncResponse
UniversalAIAsyncResponse.description:
Async response from universal-ai/async endpoint.

Inherits base fields and adds job-specific fields for tracking async operations.
Used for both job creation (202 Accepted) and job detail (GET) responses.

input.title: Input

examples.text: Analyze this text for AI detection

fallbacks.title: Fallbacks
fallbacks.description:
Fallback providers to try if the primary provider fails, in order. Accepted formats:
  - 'provider' or 'provider/model' (e.g. 'amazon', 'openai/gpt-4')
  - full model id (e.g 'text/moderation/google', 'image/generation/minimax/image-01')

show_original_response.title: Show Original Response
show_original_response.description: Include raw provider response in the output

UniversalAIBody.title: UniversalAIBody
UniversalAIBody.description:
Universal AI request body.

Model format: feature/subfeature/provider[/model]

Examples:
    - text/ai_detection/openai/gpt-4
    - ocr/ocr/amazon
    - image/generation/google/imagen-3

The `input` dict contains feature-specific parameters that are
validated at runtime based on the parsed feature/subfeature.

status.title: Status
status.description: Whether the request succeeded or failed

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: Cost in credits for this request

provider.title: Provider
provider.description: Provider name that processed the request

error.title: Error
error.description: Error details from the provider (only present when status is 'fail')

original_response.title: Original Response
original_response.description: Raw response from the provider (if show_original_response=true)

UniversalAIResponse.title: UniversalAIResponse
UniversalAIResponse.description:
Sync response from universal-ai endpoint.

Inherits all fields from base, with status restricted to success/fail.

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: Unique file identifier for use in API calls

UploadResponse.title: UploadResponse
UploadResponse.description: Response for a successful file upload.

Usage.title: Usage
Usage.description: The usage information for the request.

ImageRef.title: ImageRef
ImageRef.description:
One entry in ``images[]`` (or the ``mask`` value).

Exactly one of ``file_id`` or ``image_url`` must be set.
